task.name: Validate Workflow Output
task.description: Execute a workflow's validation checklist against its output artifact. Use when a workflow's instructions invoke validation, or when the user requests artifact validation against a checklist.
task.objective: Systematically validate a workflow output artifact against its checklist, report findings by severity, and optionally apply accepted fixes

input.name: checklist_path
input.desc: Path to the checklist file (typically {installed_path}/checklist.md from the calling workflow's validation: field)

input.name: artifact
input.desc: The artifact to validate — either a file path to load or content already in context from the calling workflow

input.name: workflow_context
input.desc: Optional workflow variables and source documents from the calling workflow (epics, architecture, etc.) for cross-reference validation

llm.i: MANDATORY: Execute ALL steps in the flow section IN EXACT ORDER
DO NOT skip steps or change the sequence
HALT immediately when halt-conditions are met
Each action xml tag within step xml tag is a REQUIRED action to complete that step
You are an independent quality validator operating in a FRESH analytical mindset
The artifact was produced by another LLM pass — assume it contains errors until proven otherwise
Be thorough but fair — flag real issues, not style preferences
Every check item in the checklist MUST be evaluated — never skip a check
Distinguish between what is wrong (critical) and what could be better (enhancement)

step.title: Load and Parse Inputs
step.action: Load the checklist file from checklist_path
HALT with error: "Checklist file not found or empty at [path]"
Load the artifact to validate (from file path or context)
HALT with error: "Artifact to validate is empty or not found"
If workflow_context was provided, note available source documents for cross-reference
Parse checklist into discrete check items — extract every checkbox (- [ ]), numbered item, or explicit validation criterion
Count total check items and announce: "Validating [artifact name] against [N] checks from [checklist name]"

step.title: Execute Checklist Systematically
step.mandate: Every check item must be evaluated — no skipping, no summarizing multiple checks as one
step.action: For each check item in the checklist, in order:

substep.title: Evaluate Check
substep.action: Examine the artifact for compliance with this specific criterion
If workflow_context is available and the check references source documents (epics, architecture, etc.), cross-reference against those documents
Record one of: PASS, FAIL, WARN, or N/A with a brief rationale

substep.title: Classify Findings
substep.action: For each non-PASS result, classify severity:
substep.finding: Missing required content, incorrect data, broken references, acceptance criteria gaps, wrong technical specifications — must be fixed before the artifact is usable
Missing optional but valuable content, incomplete coverage of edge cases, could-be-better technical detail — should be added for quality
Style improvements, token efficiency, structural reorganization, clarity tweaks — nice to have

step.title: Cross-Reference Validation
step.action: Beyond the explicit checklist, perform these universal cross-reference checks:
If source documents (epics, architecture, PRD) are available, verify the artifact's claims against them
Check for internal consistency — does the artifact contradict itself?
Check for completeness — does the artifact cover all items it claims to cover?
Check for accuracy — are names, IDs, references, and technical details correct?
Add any additional findings to the results from Step 2

step.title: Present Validation Report
step.action: Output the validation report in this format:
step.output-format:
## Validation Report
**Artifact:** [artifact name/path] **Checklist:** [checklist name/path] **Total checks:** [N] | **Pass:** [n] | **Fail:** [n] | **Warn:** [n] | **N/A:** [n]
### Check Results
| # | Check | Result | Notes | |---|-------|--------|-------| | 1 | [check description] | PASS/FAIL/WARN/N/A | [brief rationale] | | ... | ... | ... | ... |
### Critical Issues (Must Fix)
[numbered list of CRITICAL findings with specific, actionable descriptions]
### Enhancements (Should Add)
[numbered list of ENHANCEMENT findings]
### Optimizations (Nice to Have)
[numbered list of OPTIMIZATION findings]
### Summary
- **Overall:** PASS / PASS WITH WARNINGS / FAIL - **Critical issues:** [count] - **Enhancements:** [count] - **Optimizations:** [count]

step.title: Interactive Resolution
step.if: any FAIL or WARN findings exist
step.ask:
**Validation found issues. How would you like to proceed?**
- **all** — Apply all suggested fixes (critical + enhancements + optimizations) - **critical** — Apply only critical fixes - **select** — Choose specific items by number - **none** — Keep artifact as-is (acknowledge issues only) - **details** — Show more details about specific findings
Your choice:
step.action: Load the artifact file and apply the accepted changes. Make fixes look natural — the artifact should read as if it was created correctly the first time. Do NOT add meta-commentary about the validation process into the artifact. Save the updated artifact.
Acknowledge. Log that validation completed with known issues.

step.title: Completion
step.action: Report final status:
Report: "Validation complete. [N] fixes applied to [artifact path]. [remaining issues count] issues acknowledged but not fixed."

action.if: no fixes applied and no issues

step.action: Report: "Validation complete. Artifact passes all checks."

action.if: no fixes applied and issues exist

step.action: Report: "Validation complete with [N] known issues. Artifact not modified."

halt-conditions.condition: HALT if checklist file cannot be found or loaded
HALT if artifact to validate is empty or cannot be loaded
HALT if checklist contains zero parseable check items
